title: rhododendron viscosum var. ornatum
intro: Rhododendron viscosum var. ornatum, also known as the sticky azalea, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its clusters of showy, fragrant flowers and sticky stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Rhododendron viscosum var. ornatum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying the flowers and leaves, which can then be steeped in hot water or mixed with a solvent to extract its medicinal compounds.
